domain: barchan sand dune migration (crescent shape; mass-dependent migration speed where centerline is high+slow and horns are thin+fast; saltation threshold gate for wind-mobilized grains; uniform-wind crescent is steady-state of differential migration)
specific_mechanism: Differential mobility across the dune body produces self-organized crescent shape under uniform wind. Migration rate inversely proportional to mass/height. Below saltation threshold no migration.
llm_application: Differential-mobility curvature-shape stable training (DMCS): per-coordinate effective height h_i = EMA(|w_i| × |∇L w_i|); per-coordinate effective LR η_i = η_base/(1 + α·h_i). Heavily-utilized 'centerline' coordinates migrate slowly; peripheral 'horn' coordinates migrate fast. Saltation-threshold gate: coordinates with |∇L_w_i| < τ are not updated. Self-organized crescent in parameter space.
novelty_claim: Width-scaled LR (2505.22491), weight decay > muP (2510.19093), scaling-collapse (2509.25087) cover global LR. None retrieve per-coordinate mass-and-threshold-gated mobility producing self-organized crescent.
